seed: spark vs ash; hunger_vs_restraint; diagonal_argument; wave_vertical; radial against split_screen; jagged_edges; memory_vs_erasure; star; resilience_fragility | abstract variation request 259; target batch count 500
title: Resilience and Fragility
reasoning: This artwork embodies the emotional dichotomy of 'spark' versus 'ash', illustrating the tension between sudden brightness and a soft residue. The chosen palette of off-white, dust gray, compressed red, dark navy, and faint peach reflects a balance of quiet pressure and vibrant interruption. The layout, reminiscent of a hollow center ringed by contrasting colors, signifies the inner conflict between desire for fulfillment and restraint. Main contrast occurs in the center where jagged red cracks (representing sparks of intensity) interrupt a field of muted gray and navy, illustrating a collision of memories and emotions. The broken pixels enhance the sense of imperfection, suggesting resilience through fragments of recollection. Overall, the piece captures the push and pull of emotional experiences, evoking a sense of unresolved tension between opposing forces.

layout.name: halo_void
layout.description: A hollow center is ringed by light, shadow, or static.

composition.name: repair_vs_damage
composition.idea: Broken bands are visibly patched by warmer or steadier pixels.

referenceExample.title: Rage Entering Calm
referenceExample.pattern: red jagged pixels cutting into blue horizontal bands

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
